summary: Mapped the full Pantry-relevant architecture. The app is a single-household Next/SQLite service architecture with structured but non-canonical recipe ingredients, dimension-limited scaling helpers, server-side meal planning, snapshot shopping lists, and profile-specific cook sessions. No product, batch, location, event, mapping, allocation, override, purchase-intake, or deduction layer exists. Lint, types, 50 unit tests, 30 integration tests, OpenAPI, and build pass; checkout-wide Prettier is pre-existing Windows noise. The recommended first slice is an additive, event-backed Pantry vertical slice with optimistic batch versioning, trusted APIs, native UI, tests, and docs.
facts: One physical database represents one household; profiles are signed attribution preferences, not access control.
Recipe ingredients preserve structured quantity/unit/item/note but have no canonical identity or optional flag.
Generated shopping lists aggregate exact raw unit/item/note keys into independent editable snapshots and retain only source recipe IDs.
Cook completion has no planned-meal link or inventory transaction.
The UI uses server components plus local React state/fetch, shared plain CSS primitives, and no global client state library.
Current green gates: lint, typecheck, 50 unit tests, 30 integration tests, OpenAPI valid with warnings, and production build.
contradictions: pnpm verify is red only because checkout-wide Prettier reports 95 unchanged Windows-format files; formatting must be separated from Pantry-owned diffs.
The brief asks for household isolation, while repository architecture deliberately provides one trusted household per deployment rather than multi-tenant authorization.
ambiguity_requiring_judge: Choose the exact boundary of the first inventory vertical slice, especially split/combine/donation/undo UI.
Decide whether singleton household ownership or explicit household_id best preserves current architecture.
Schedule planned-meal status and optional product images without deferring full acceptance.

commands.summary: Stopped at pre-existing checkout-wide Prettier warnings across 95 unchanged files.
Production build passed with one existing backup NFT trace warning.
